Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.\n// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.\n\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.ComponentModel;\nusing System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;\nusing System.Linq.Expressions;\nusing System.Reflection;\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.HotReload;\n\nnamespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Forms;\n\ninternal static class ExpressionMemberAccessor\n{\n    private static readonly ConcurrentDictionary<Expression, MemberInfo> _memberInfoCache = new();\n    private static readonly ConcurrentDictionary<MemberInfo, string> _displayNameCache = new();\n\n    static ExpressionMemberAccessor()\n    {\n        if (HotReloadManager.Default.MetadataUpdateSupported)\n        {\n            HotReloadManager.Default.OnDeltaApplied += ClearCache;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemberInfo GetMemberInfo<TValue>(Expression<Func<TValue>> accessor)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(accessor);\n\n        return _memberInfoCache.GetOrAdd(accessor, static expr =>\n        {\n            var lambdaExpression = (LambdaExpression)expr;\n            var accessorBody = lambdaExpression.Body;\n\n            if (accessorBody is UnaryExpression unaryExpression\n                && unaryExpression.NodeType == ExpressionType.Convert\n                && unaryExpression.Type == typeof(object))\n            {\n                accessorBody = unaryExpression.Operand;\n            }\n\n            if (accessorBody is not MemberExpression memberExpression)\n            {\n                throw new ArgumentException(\n                    $\"The provided expression contains a {accessorBody.GetType().Name} which is not supported. \" +\n                    $\"Only simple member accessors (fields, properties) of an object are supported.\");\n            }\n\n            return memberExpression.Member;\n        });\n    }\n\n    public static string GetDisplayName(MemberInfo member)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(member);\n\n        return _displayNameCache.GetOrAdd(member, static m =>\n        {\n            var displayNameAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayNameAttribute>();\n            if (displayNameAttribute?.DisplayName is not null)\n            {\n                return displayNameAttribute.DisplayName;\n            }\n\n            var displayAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayAttribute>();\n            if (displayAttribute is not null)\n            {\n                var name = displayAttribute.GetName();\n                if (name is not null)\n                {\n                    return name;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return m.Name;\n        });\n    }\n\n    public static string GetDisplayName<TValue>(Expression<Func<TValue>> accessor)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(accessor);\n        var member = GetMemberInfo(accessor);\n        return GetDisplayName(member);\n    }\n\n    private static void ClearCache()\n    {\n        _memberInfoCache.Clear();\n    }\n}\n","numLines":91,"startLine":1,"totalLines":91}}}

However, this feels more like a design flaw than a critical defect—I'd rate it HIGH severity rather than CRITICAL.\n\nI've identified three defects total. Now I need to be precise about line numbers when reporting them. For the `ClearCache` incomplete issue, the problem spans lines 86-89, so I should reference line 88 where the missing statement should be.\n\nThe attribute precedence problem starts at line 59 where the incorrect check begins. And the missing optimization in `SetParametersAsync` occurs at line 35 where the method fails to check if parameters actually changed before re-rendering. With these line references established, I can now construct the JSON report.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":13,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2203,"cache_read_input_tokens":21601,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2203},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b003dcf7-2097-4e1b-8d41-640de51b4f10","uuid":"20e00909-9beb-449c-81f4-f8c1fe55be32"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01U341prhyNFUuyegGjgU3At","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good, so this is the commit that added this feature. Now let me analyze the code carefully for defects.\n\nLooking at the code:\n\n### DEFECT 1: Incomplete cache clearing\nIn `ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs`, line 86-89:\n```csharp\nprivate static void ClearCache()\n{\n    _memberInfoCache.Clear();\n}\n```\n\nThe `ClearCache()` method is called when hot reload is triggered (line 22). However, it only clears `_memberInfoCache` but NOT `_displayNameCache`. This means:\n- When metadata is updated (e.g., attributes are changed), the member info cache is cleared\n- But the display name cache still contains old values\n- Subsequent calls to `GetDisplayName` will return stale cached display names\n\nThis is a CRITICAL functional bug.\n\n### DEFECT 2: Incorrect attribute precedence\nIn `ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs`, lines 59-73:\nThe code checks `[DisplayName]` FIRST:\n```csharp\nvar displayNameAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayNameAttribute>();\nif (displayNameAttribute?.DisplayName is not null)\n{\n    return displayNameAttribute.DisplayName;\n}\n\nvar displayAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayAttribute>();\n...\n```\n\nBut ASP.NET Core conventions dictate that `[Display]` (from DataAnnotations) should take precedence over `[DisplayName]` (from ComponentModel) because:\n1. `[Display]` is the modern, standard data annotation attribute in ASP.NET Core\n2. `[DisplayName]` is legacy from System.ComponentModel\n3. The documentation comment lists \"DisplayAttribute\" first, implying that should be the priority\n\nThe current code gives precedence to the older attribute, which is backwards.\n\nThis is a HIGH functional bug.\n\n### DEFECT 3: Missing optimization with unused field\nIn `DisplayName.cs`, line 20 declares `_previousFieldAccessor` but it's never used. Meanwhile, in `SetParametersAsync` (lines 35-44), the component always calls `_renderHandle.Render()` without checking if the `For` expression actually changed.\n\n\nThe unused field indicates an incomplete optimization—the code should track whether the `For` parameter changed by reference and only trigger re-renders when necessary. This is a standard Blazor performance pattern that's missing here, causing unnecessary rendering cycles.\n\nThis is a HIGH compliance violation.\n\nLet me create the review.json file with these defects and their fixes.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7517,"cache_read_input_tokens":23804,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":7517},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b003dcf7-2097-4e1b-8d41-640de51b4f10","uuid":"d06f2aa5-f31a-4008-b26f-17ab1a557dde"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01U341prhyNFUuyegGjgU3At","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I'll analyze both files for defects. Let me examine the code carefully:\n\n## Analysis\n\nI've identified three defects:\n\n### Defect 1: Incomplete Cache Clearing (Critical)\nIn `ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs`, the `ClearCache()` method (lines 86-89) only clears `_memberInfoCache` but not `_displayNameCache`. During hot reload, if display name attributes change on a type, the cached display names will become stale because the display name cache is never cleared.\n\n### Defect 2: Incorrect Attribute Precedence (High)\nIn `ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs` (lines 59-73), the code checks `[DisplayName]` before `[Display]`. However, ASP.NET Core conventions dictate that `[Display]` (the modern DataAnnotations attribute) should take precedence over `[DisplayName]` (the legacy ComponentModel attribute). The documentation comment even lists \"DisplayAttribute\" first, implying it should be checked first.\n\n### Defect 3: Missing Optimization with Unused Field (High)\nIn `DisplayName.cs`, the `_previousFieldAccessor` field (line 20) is declared but never used. The `SetParametersAsync` method (lines 35-44) always renders without checking if the `For` expression changed. The presence of this unused field indicates an incomplete optimization where the component should only re-render when the `For` expression changes by reference (a standard Blazor component optimization).\n\nNow I'll create the review report:"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7517,"cache_read_input_tokens":23804,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":7517},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b003dcf7-2097-4e1b-8d41-640de51b4f10","uuid":"508c0b00-89ba-4b06-9c08-00dd58a34ddf"}
